content.claim: Hungarian primary teachers earn 34% less than tertiary-educated workers (OECD comparison: 17% less), and a 32% average salary increase was implemented in early 2024, but Hungary continues to face severe teacher shortage and aging workforce.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Portuguese system reform lens
limitations: Recent salary increase timing and long-term retention effects not yet measured
Does not establish whether salary alone resolves recruitment to rural areas
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
